report_name: CrowdStrike Patches Critical Flaw (CVE-2026-40050) in Self-Hosted LogScale Platform

vulnerabilities.name: CVE-2026-40050
vulnerabilities.description: A critical, unauthenticated path traversal vulnerability (CWE-22) in a cluster API endpoint of self-hosted CrowdStrike LogScale allows a remote attacker to read arbitrary files from the server's filesystem.

identities.name: CrowdStrike
identities.description: Vendor of the LogScale platform who discovered and patched the vulnerability CVE-2026-40050.

indicators.name: CrowdStrike LogScale Path Traversal Attempt (CVE-2026-40050)
indicators.pattern:
title: CrowdStrike LogScale Path Traversal Attempt (CVE-2026-40050)
id: 3b8c1f9a-e5d4-4f9a-8a1b-9c2d3e4f5a6b
status: experimental
description: 'Detects potential exploitation attempts of the path traversal vulnerability CVE-2026-40050 in CrowdStrike LogScale by looking for common traversal sequences in web request URIs.'
references:
  - https://securityaffairs.co/162310/hacking/critical-bug-in-crowdstrike-logscale-let-attackers-access-files.html
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-04-27
tags:
  - attack.t1190
  - attack.t1083
falsepositives:
  - Legitimate URLs that happen to contain path traversal-like sequences, though this is rare for API endpoints.
level: high
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    c-uri|contains:
      - '../'
      - '..\'
      - '..%2f'
  condition: selection
indicators.description: This indicator detects web requests containing path traversal sequences, which could indicate an attempt to exploit CVE-2026-40050 against a CrowdStrike LogScale instance.
